Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth

Class AuthFilterChain

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.AuthFilterChain
All Implemented Interfaces:
jakarta.servlet.Filter, Authenticator
public class AuthFilterChain extends Object implements jakarta.servlet.Filter, Authenticator
Orchestrates multiple AuthFilter instances with first-success principal resolution, role aggregation, and all-failure aggregation semantics.

Chain decision flow per request:

  1. Build the set of matching filters (those whose UrlPathMatcher pattern matches the request path, or those registered without a pattern which always match).
  2. If no filters match the path, the request passes through unchanged.
  3. Iterate matching filters in declaration order, calling Authenticator.authenticate(HttpServletRequest):
    • Optional.empty() — filter doesn't apply this request; continue.
    • Optional.of(AuthResult) — success. The first successful filter's Principal wins for identity. All subsequent successful filters contribute their roles to the union.
    • throw AuthenticationException — credentials present but invalid; record as a failure.
  4. After iterating:
    • If at least one filter succeeded — wrap the request in AuthenticatedRequestWrapper with the first-success principal and union role set; delegate to the next filter/servlet.
    • If no filter succeeded but at least one threw — aggregate the WWW-Authenticate challenges and send a 401 response.
    • If all matching filters returned empty (no credentials recognized) — pass through unchanged.

AuthFilterChain itself implements Filter so it can be registered with a servlet container as a single filter at "/*" and delegate internally to its composed AuthFilter entries.

Registration

The preferred registration path is via @Bean; JettyServerComponent auto-mounts an AuthFilterChain bean at "/*":

@Bean public AuthFilterChain authFilters(BeanStore bs) { return AuthFilterChain.create(bs) .append(BearerTokenAuthFilter.create().pattern("/api/*").validator(jwtValidator).build()) .append(ApiKeyAuthFilter.create().pattern("/api/*").store(apiKeyStore).build()) .build(); }

See Also:
  • AuthFilter
  • AuthResult
  • AuthenticatedRequestWrapper
  • AuthN Filter Framework
Since:
10.0.0

      See the class-level Javadoc for the full decision flow.

      Specified by:
      doFilter in interface jakarta.servlet.Filter
      Throws:
      IOException
      jakarta.servlet.ServletException

    • authenticate

      public Optional<AuthResult> authenticate(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest hreq) throws AuthenticationException
      Runs the composed AuthFilter entries for the request and folds their results, without writing any response.

      Selects the entries whose path pattern matches the request (or that have no pattern), runs each in declaration order folding results via an AuthResultAccumulator (first-success principal wins, roles union), and:

      • returns the folded AuthResult if any entry succeeded;
      • throws an aggregated AuthenticationException (combining all WWW-Authenticate challenges) if no entry succeeded but at least one rejected the request;
      • returns Optional.empty() if no entry matched the path or all matching entries returned empty.
      Specified by:
      authenticate in interface Authenticator
      Parameters:
      hreq - The incoming HTTP request. Never null.
      Returns:
      The folded authentication result, or Optional.empty().
      Throws:
      AuthenticationException - If no entry succeeded but at least one rejected the request.
